54.3: For strangers are risen up against me, and the mighty seek after my life; they have disregarded thee, O God.
54.4: Behold, God is my helper; the LORD sustains my soul.
54.5: Bring disaster to mine enemies; silence them in thy truth.
54.6: I will freely sacrifice to thee; I will praise thy name, O LORD, for it is good;
54.7: For thou hast delivered me out of all troubles; and mine eye hath seen mine enemies defeated.

55.1: GIVE ear to my prayer, O God; and reject not my supplication.
55.2: Hear me and answer me; return to my cry and incline to me,
55.3: Because of mine enemies, because of the oppression of the wicked; for they have devised iniquity against me, and reproached me.
55.4: My heart is greatly pained within me; and the terrors of death are fallen upon me.
55.5: Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and the shadow of death has overwhelmed me.
55.6: And I said, Oh that I had wings like a dove! Then would I fly away and be at rest.
55.7: Lo, then I would fly far off and dwell in the wilderness.
55.8: I would wait for him that will save me from the windy storm and the tempest.
55.9: Destroy, O LORD, and render useless their tongues; for I have seen violence and strife in the city.
55.10: Day and night they go about it, around its walls; injustice and mischief are in the midst of it.
55.11: Wickedness is in the midst of it; deceit and guile depart not from its streets.
55.12: For it was not mine enemy that reproached me; then I could have borne it; neither was it he that hated me that did magnify himself against me; then I would have hid myself from him;
55.13: But it was you, a man mine equal, my kinsman, and my friend.
55.14: We ate a meal together in the house of God, and we walked in harmony.
55.15: Bring death upon them; bury them alive in the grave, because there is evil among them.
55.16: As for me, I will call upon God; and God shall save me.
55.17: At evening and in the morning and at noon will I pray and cry aloud; and he shall hear my voice.
55.18: Deliver my soul from those who have been too wise for me, from those who have opposed me continuously.
55.19: God, even the Eternal one, shall hear and humble them. With them also there are no changes; therefore they fear not God.
55.20: They have put forth their hands against their neighbours, they have broken his covenant.
55.21: They were afraid because of the anger in his countenance and the wrath in his heart; his words were smoother than butter, yet were they sharp like the point of a spear.
55.22: Cast your worries upon the LORD and he will sustain you; he will never suffer the righteous to fear want.
55.23: But thou, O God, shalt bring them down into the pit of destruction; bloody and deceitful men shall not live out their days; but I will trust in thee.

56.1: BE merciful to me, O God; for man has trodden me under foot; all the day long the fighting oppresses me.
56.2: Mine enemies have daily trodden me under foot; for many warriors have risen against me.
56.3: I fear not during the day, because I trust in thee.
56.4: In God I will be glorified, in God I have put my trust; I will not fear what man can do to me.
56.5: All the day long they conspired against me and they devised evil against me.
56.6: They will wait in hiding, they will watch my steps, inasmuch as they wish for my death.
56.7: He has no deliverer, they have said. Condemn them with the condemnation of the Gentiles.
56.8: O God, I have declared my faith unto thee; record thou my tears before thee in thy book.
56.9: Then shall mine enemies turn back; and I will know I have a God.
56.10: In God will I praise his word; in the LORD will I praise his word.
56.11: In God have I put my trust; I will not be afraid what man can do to me.
56.12: To thee shall I perform my vows, O God; I will offer them to thee with thank offering.
56.13: For thou hast delivered my soul from death and my feet from stumbling, that I may be pleasing in thy sight, O God, in the land of the living.

57.1: BE merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me; for my soul trusts in thee; yea, in the shadow of thy wings will I make my refuge until these calamities be overpast.
57.2: I will cry to God most High, to God my Saviour.
57.3: For he has sent from heaven and delivered me; he has put mine enemies to shame. God shall send forth his mercy and his truth.
57.4: He has delivered my life from the vicious as I slept in fear, and from the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongues sharp swords.
57.5: Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; let thy glory be above all the earth.
57.6: They have prepared a net for my feet; they have digged a pit for my soul, into the midst of which they themselves have fallen.
57.7: My heart is made ready, O God, my heart is ready; I will sing and give praise in my glory.
57.8: Awake, my harp; awake, psaltery and harp; I myself will awake early.
57.9: I will praise thee, O LORD, among the people; I will sing unto thy name among the nations.
57.10: For thy mercy is exalted to the sky and thy faithfulness to the heavens.
57.11: Be thou exalted, O God, above the heavens; let thy glory be above all the earth.

58.1: DO you indeed speak righteousness. O congregation? do you judge uprightly, O sons of men?
58.2: Behold you all speak evil on earth, and your hands are soiled with injustice.
58.3: The wicked are known from the womb; they go astray as soon as they are born, speaking lies.
58.4: Their poison is like the poison of a serpent; they are like the deaf adder that stops its ear,
58.5: And will not listen to the voice of the whisperer, neither to the charmer nor to the wise.
58.6: Break their teeth in their mouths, O God; pull out the fangs of the lions, O LORD.
58.7: Let them be rejected as water which is contaminated; let God shoot his arrows until the wicked are destroyed.
58.8: Like the wax that melts, and drips before the fire, let them be destroyed: fire has fallen from heaven and they did not see; the light of truth has been given and they did not understand.
58.9: Let their thorns be increased, and fear of wrath shake them violently.
58.10: The righteous shall rejoice when he sees the vengeance: he shall wash his hands in the blood of the wicked,
58.11: So that a man shall say, Verily there is a reward for the righteous; verily there is a God that judges in the earth.

59.1: DELIVER me from mine enemies, O God; defend me from them that rise up against me.
59.2: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from bloody men.
59.3: For, lo, they lie in wait for my soul; evil have they multiplied against me; not because I have transgressed or because I have sinned, O LORD.
59.4: They run and prepare themselves against me without considering whether I have been at fault;
59.5: Awake to help me, and behold, O thou LORD God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the nations, and spare not any wicked transgressors.
59.6: They return at evening; they make a noise like dogs, and go round about the city.
59.7: Behold, they belch out with their mouths; swords are in their lips; for who, say they, does care?
59.8: But thou, O LORD, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the nations in derision.
59.9: To thee shall I give praise, O God, for thou art my defense.
59.10: O God, thy lovingkindness has preceded me; God has let me live to see my enemies avenged.
59.11: Spare them for a living example, lest my people forget; but scatter them by thy power, and bring them down, O LORD my hope.
59.12: For the sin of their mouth and the words of their lips. let them even be taken in their pride; for it is cursing and lying that they speak.
59.13: Consume them in thy wrath, consume them, that they be no more, that they may know that God rules over Jacob and over the ends of the earth.
59.14: And at evening let them return; and let them make a noise like dogs, and go round about the city.
59.15: Let them be hungry and not be filled, neither let them find a lodging place.
59.16: But I will sing of thy power; yea, I will sing aloud of thy mercy in the morning; for thou hast been my defense and refuge in the day of my trouble.
59.17: Unto thee, O God, will I sing; for thou art God of my refuge and the God of my mercy.

60.1: O GOD, thou hast forsaken us, thou hast scattered us, thou hast been displeased with us; O turn thyself to us again.
60.2: Thou hast made the earth to tremble; thou hast broken it; heal the breaches thereof, for it hath weakened.
60.3: Thou hast shown thy people hardships; thou hast made us to drink the dregs of wine.
60.4: Thou hast wrought a miracle to them that reverence thee, so that they need not flee from the bow.
60.5: That thy beloved may be prepared, save with thy right hand, and hear me.
60.6: God has spoken in his holiness: I will be strong, I will divide Shechem and mete out the valley of Succoth.
60.7: Gilead is mine and Manasseh is mine; Ephraim also is the strength of my head; Judah is my king.
60.8: Moab is my washpot; over Edom will I levy tribute; over Philistia will I triumph.
60.9: Who will lead me into Edom? Who will bring me into the strong city?
60.10: For behold, O God, thou hadst cast us off, and thou didst not go before us with our armies.
60.11: Give us strength against our enemies; for vain is the help of man.
60.12: Through God we shall do valiantly; for he it is who shall tread down our enemies.

61.1: HEAR my cry, O God; attend to my prayer.
61.2: From the end of the earth will I cry unto thee, when my heart is overwhelmed; for thou hast led me upon a rock and hast comforted me.
61.3: For thou hast been a shelter for me, and a strong tower from my enemies,
61.4: I will abide in thy tabernacle for ever; I will make my refuge the shadow of thy wings.
61.5: For thou, O God, hast heard my vows; thou hast given an inheritance to them that revere thy name.
61.6: Thou hast added days to the days of the king, and his years as many generations.
61.7: He shall be established before God for ever; mercy and justice shall preserve him.
61.8: So will I sing praise unto thy name for ever, when daily I fulfil my vows.

62.1: MY soul waits upon God; from him comes my salvation.
62.2: He only is my God and my salvation; he is my great defense; I shall not be moved.
62.3: How long will you threaten a man so that you may kill him? Like a crumbling wall shall you be and as a tottering fence.
62.4: For you have thought to cast him down from his excellency; you delight in lies; you bless with your mouths, but curse with your hearts.
62.5: My soul, wait only upon God; for my salvation is from him.
62.6: He only is my God and my salvation; he is my defense; I shall not be moved.
62.7: In God is my salvation and my glory; God is my strength, my refuge, and my hope.
62.8: Trust in him at all times; you people, pour out your hearts before him; God is a refuge for us.
62.9: Surely all untrue men are like vapor; when they are placed in the balance, they are found wanting.
62.10: Trust not in oppression, and become not vain in robbery; if riches increase, let not your heart rejoice over them.
62.11: God has said this once; twice have I heard this; that power belongs unto God.
62.12: Also unto thee, O LORD, belongs mercy; for thou renderest to every man according to his work.

63.1: O GOD, thou art my God; on thee I wait; my soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh longeth for thee as in a dry and thirsty land where no water is.
63.2: As, in purity, I have seen thee, so may I see thy power and thy glory.
63.3: Because thy lovingkindness is better than life, my lips shall praise thee.
63.4: Thus will I bless thee while I live; I will lift up my hands in thy name.
63.5: My soul shall be enriched as with cream and fatness; and my mouth shall praise thee with joyful lips.
63.6: I remember thee upon my bed and I meditate on thee in the night watches.
63.7: Because thou hast been my helper, therefore in the shadow of thy wings will I glory.
63.8: My soul follows hard after thee; thy right hand upholds me.
63.9: But those that seek my soul, to destroy it, shall go into the lower parts of the earth.
63.10: They shall fall by the sword; they shall be a portion for foxes.
63.11: But the king shall rejoice in God; every man that swears by him shall glory; but the mouths of those who speak lies shall be stopped.

64.1: HEAR my voice, O God, as I pray to thee; preserve my life from fear of mine enemy.
64.2: Hide me from the wickedness of the evil one; from the corruption of the workers of iniquity,
64.3: Who whet their tongues like a sword, and charge their words with poison like an arrow,
64.4: That they may shoot in secret at the innocent; suddenly do they shoot at him, and fear not.
64.5: They encourage themselves with evil speech; they plan to hide snares; they say, Who shall see us?
64.6: They plan wickedness, but they are destroyed in order to consume evil from among men and from the depths of the heart.
64.7: But God is great; suddenly he shall shoot at them with an arrow;
64.8: So shall their tongues be silenced, and all that see them shall be frightened.
64.9: And all men shall fear, and shall declare the work of God; for they shall wisely consider the work of his hands.
64.10: The righteous shall be glad in the LORD, and shall trust in him; and all the upright in heart shall glorify him.

65.1: PRAISE is befitting thee, O God, in Zion; and unto thee shall the vow be performed;
65.2: O hear my prayer; unto thee shall all flesh come.
65.3: The words of the wicked have prevailed against me; as for our transgressions, thou shalt purge them.
65.4: Blessed is the man in whom thou art pleased, he whom thou causest to approach unto thee, that he may dwell in thy courts and be satisfied with the goodness of thy house, even of thy holy temple.
65.5: From the majesty of thy righteousness answer us, O God of our salvation, who art the confidence of all the ends of the earth, and of the peoples who are afar off,
65.6: Who settest fast the mountains by his strength, and is mighty in his power;
65.7: He stills the tempest of the seas and the roar of their waves. The peoples shall be troubled.
65.8: The inhabitants of the earth shall tremble at thy wonders, from the coming of the morning to the evening.
65.9: With glory didst thou visit the earth and water it; thou hast blessed it with peace and hast greatly enriched it with the river of God, which is full of water; thou hast prepared food when thou hast established it.
65.10: Thou hast watered the fallowed ground thereof; to bring forth fruits, thou hast sprinkled its growth with showers and blessed it.
65.11: Thou crownest the year with thy goodness; and the calves have rich pasture.
65.12: They shall be filled with the pastures of the wilderness, and the hills shall be surrounded with glory.
65.13: The pastures shall be covered with fattening sheep, the valleys shall be clothed with wheat; they shall rejoice, yea, they shall sing.

66.1: MAKE a joyful noise unto God, all lands.
66.2: Sing the honor of his name; sing the honor of his majesty.
66.3: Say unto God, How wonderful are thy works! Through the greatness of thy power shall thine enemies submit themselves unto thee.
66.4: All the earth shall worship thee, and shall sing unto thy name; they shall glorify thy name forever.
66.5: They shall say, Come and see the works of God; for his wonders are great toward the children of men.
66.6: He turns the sea into dry land: they crossed through the river on foot; there did we rejoice in him.
66.7: He rules by his power for ever; his eyes behold the nations; let not the rebellious exalt themselves.
66.8: O bless our God, you people, and make the voice of his praise to be heard,
66.9: Who holds our soul in life, and suffers not our feet to be moved.
66.10: For thou, O God, hast proved us; thou hast tried us as silver is tried.
66.11: Thou didst bring us into the net; thou didst lay affliction upon our loins.
66.12: Thou hast caused us to be in servitude; we went through fire and through water; finally thou didst bring us out into a comfortable place.
66.13: I will go into thy house with gifts; I will pay thee my vows.
66.14: Which my lips have uttered and my mouth has spoken when I was in trouble.
66.15: I will offer unto thee burnt sacrifices of fatlings, with the savour of rams; I will offer bullocks with goats.
66.16: Come and hear, all you servants of God, and I will declare what he has done for my soul.
66.17: I cried to him with my mouth, and he has answered me; I extolled him with my tongue.
66.18: If thou behold iniquity in my heart, O LORD, deliver me not.
66.19: But verily God has heard me; he has attended to the voice of my prayer.
66.20: Blessed be God, who has not rejected my prayer nor turned his mercy from me.

67.1: GOD be merciful unto us and bless us; and cause his face to shine upon us,
67.2: That thy ways may be known upon earth, thy saving health among all nations.
67.3: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.
67.4: O let the nations be glad and sing for joy; for thou shalt judge the people righteously, and govern the nations upon earth.
67.5: Let the people praise thee, O God; let all the people praise thee.
67.6: Then shall the earth yield her increase; and God, even our own God, shall bless us.
67.7: God shall bless us; and all the ends of the earth shall fear him.

68.1: LET God arise, let his enemies be scattered; let them also that hate him flee before him.
68.2: As smoke is driven away, so let them vanish; as wax melts before the fire, so let the wicked perish at the presence of God.
68.3: But let the righteous be glad; let them be valiant in the sight of God; yea, let them rejoice in his pleasure.
68.4: Sing unto God, sing praises to his name; extol him who rides upon the heavens; the LORD is his name, rejoice before him.
68.5: A father of the fatherless and a judge of widows is God in his holy habitation.
68.6: God sets the solitary child in families; he triumphantly releases those who are bound with chains; but the rebellious dwell in waste places.
68.7: O God, when thou didst go forth before thy people, when thou didst march through the wilderness,
68.8: The earth shook, the heavens also lowered at the presence of God; even Sinai itself was moved at the presence of God, the God of Israel.
68.9: Thou, O God, didst send a plentiful rain whereby thou didst confirm thine inheritance when it was weary.
68.10: Thy living creatures have dwelt therein; thou O God, hast provided of thy goodness for the poor.
68.11: The LORD shall give good tidings with great power.
68.12: Then the kings of armies shall be defeated; and the household of God shall divide the spoil.
68.13: Though you sleep among thorns, yet shall you be protected as the wings of a dove covered with silver, and her feathers with yellow gold.
68.14: When God appointed a king over Zion, it became white like snow
68.15: Over Salmon, the mountain of God, over the mountains of Bashan and over the mountain ridges.
68.16: What do you want, O you mountains of Bashan? This is the ridge which God desires to dwell in; yea, the LORD will dwell in it for ever.
68.17: God rides with the host of angels; the LORD is among them, as in Sinai, in his holy place.
68.18: Thou hast ascended on high, thou hast carried away captives; thou hast blessed men with gifts; but rebellious men shall not dwell before the presence of God.
68.19: Blessed be the LORD, for he has chosen us as his heritage, even the God of our salvation.
68.20: He that is our God is the God of salvation, the LORD God who has the power of life and death.
68.21: But God shall sever the head of his enemies, and the hairy scalp of the head of those who persist in their sins.
68.22: The LORD said, I will rescue my people from the cliffs, I will bring my people from the depths of the sea;
68.23: That thy foot shall be dipped in the blood of thine enemies, and the tongue of thy dogs in the same.
68.24: They have seen thy goings, O God, even the goings of my God and my holy King.
68.25: The princes went before, the singers followed after; among them were the damsels playing with timbrels.
68.26: Bless God in the congregations, even the LORD, you that are of the fountain of Israel.
68.27: There is little Benjamin with their ruler, the princes of Judah and their governors, the princes of Zebulun, and the princes of Naphtali.
68.28: Command, O God, thy strength; strengthen, O God, that which thou hast wrought for us.
68.29: Out of thy temple at Jerusalem shall kings bring presents unto thee.
68.30: Rebuke the wild beasts of the marshes, the multitude of the wild bulls, the idols of the Gentiles which are covered with silver; scatter the people who delight in war.
68.31: Ambassadors shall come out of Egypt; Ethiopia shall soon stretch out her hands to God.
68.32: Sing to God, you kingdoms of the earth; O sing praises to the LORD.
68.33: To him who rides upon the heavens of heavens; out of the east he gives his voice, and that a mighty voice.
68.34: Ascribe glory unto God; his excellency is over Israel and his strength is in the heavens.
68.35: O God, thou art honoured out of thy holy place; the God of Israel is he that gives strength and power to his people. Blessed be God.

69.1: SAVE me, O God; for the waters are come near to engulf me.
69.2: I sink in deep mire where there is no footing; I am come into deep waters, where the floods overflow me.
69.3: I am weary of my crying; my throat is dry; mine eyes fail while I wait for God.
69.4: They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head; they that would destroy me, being mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty; then I restored that which I took lawfully.
69.5: O God, thou knowest my foolishness; and my sins are not hid from thee.
69.6: Let not them that trust in thee be ashamed through me, O LORD God of hosts; let not those that seek thee be dishonored through me, O God of Israel.
69.7: Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; shame has covered my face.
69.8: I am become a stranger to my brethren and an alien to my mother's children.
69.9: For the zeal of thy house has made me courageous; and the reproaches of them that reproached thee are fallen upon me.
69.10: When I humbled and chastened my soul with fasting, that was to my reproach.
69.11: I made sackcloth also my garment and I became a proverb to them.
69.12: They that sit in the gate speak against me; and I was the song of the drunkards.
69.13: But as for me, my prayer is unto thee, O LORD, in an acceptable time; O God, in the multitude of thy mercy answer me, in the abundance of thy salvation,
69.14: Deliver me out of the mire, and let me not sink; let me be delivered from them that hate me, and out of the deep waters.
69.15: Let not the waterflood overflow me, neither let the deep swallow me up, and let not the well shut its mouth upon me.
69.16: Answer me, O LORD; for thy lovingkindness is good; turn to me according to the multitude of thy tender mercies.
69.17: And hide not thy face from thy servant; for I am in trouble; answer me speedily.
69.18: Draw near to my soul, and redeem it; deliver me because of mine enemies.
69.19: Thou knowest my reproach and my shame, and my dishonour is spread before all mine adversaries.
69.20: O thou, heal my broken heart and bind it; I looked for some to take pity, but there was none; and for comforters, but I found none.
69.21: They gave me bitter herbs for my food; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
69.22: Let their table become a snare before them; and that which should have been for their reward, let it become a trap.
69.23: Let their eyes be darkened, that they see not; and make their backs continually to bend.
69.24: Pour out thine indignation upon them, and let thy wrathful anger take hold of them.
69.25: Let their habitation be desolate; and let none dwell in their tents.
69.26: The one which thou hast smitten, they have persecuted; they have added to the grief of him who is wounded.
69.27: Add punishment of iniquity to their iniquity, that they may enter not into thy righteousness.
69.28: Let them be blotted out of thy book of the living, and not be written with the righteous.
69.29: But I am poor and sorrowful; let thy salvation help me, O God.
69.30: I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.
69.31: This shall please the LORD better than an ox or bullock that has horns and hoofs.
69.32: The humble shall see this and be glad; and your heart shall live that seek God.
69.33: For the LORD hears the poor, and despises not his prisoners.
69.34: Let heaven and earth praise him, the seas, and every thing that moves therein.
69.35: For God will save Zion, and will build the cities of Judah;
69.36: His servants shall dwell in it and shall inherit it; they that love his name shall dwell therein.

70.1: MAKE haste, O God, to deliver me; make haste to help me, O LORD.
70.2: Let them be ashamed and confounded that seek after my soul: let them be turned backward and put to confusion that desire my hurt.
70.3: Let them be confounded by their recurring shame that say to me, Aha, aha!
70.4: Let all those that seek thee rejoice and be glad in thee; and let such as love thy salvation say continually, Let God be magnified.
70.5: But I am poor and needy; make haste unto me, O God; thou art my help and my deliverer; O LORD, tarry not.

71.1: IN thee, O LORD, do I put my trust; let me never be confounded.
71.2: Deliver me in thy righteousness and rescue me; incline thine ear to me and save me.
71.3: Be thou to me a sheltering rock whereunto I may continually resort; give thou commandment to save me; for thou art my rock and my fortress.
71.4: Deliver me, O God, out of the hand of the wicked, out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
71.5: For thou art my hope, O LORD; thou art my trust from my youth, O God.
71.6: By thee have I been sustained from the womb; thou art my hope since I was in my mother's body; my praise is continually of thee.
71.7: I am as a wonder to many; but thou art my strong refuge.
71.8: Let my mouth be filled with thy praise and with thy honor all the day.
71.9: Cast me not off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength fails.
71.10: For mine enemies speak against me; and they that lie in wait for my soul take counsel together,
71.11: Saying, God has forsaken him; pursue and take him; for there is none to deliver him.
71.12: O God, be not far from me; O my God, make haste for my help.
71.13: Let them be ashamed and embarrassed who envy my soul: let them be covered with shame that seek my hurt.
71.14: But I will pray continually, and will praise thee yet more and more.
71.15: My mouth shall show forth thy righteousness and thy salvation all the day, for I cannot read.
71.16: I will go in the strength of the LORD; I will make mention of thy righteousness even of thine only.
71.17: O God, thou hast taught me from my youth, so that I might declare thy wondrous works.
71.18: Now also to old age and gray hairs, O God, forsake me not; until I have shown thy strength and thy might to the generation that is to come.
71.19: Thy righteousness also, O God, has reached to the highest; thou hast done great things; O God, who is like unto thee!
71.20: Thou who hast shown me great and sore troubles, shalt quicken me again, and shalt bring me up again from the depths of the earth.
71.21: Thou hast increased my greatness, and hast returned to comfort me.
71.22: I will also praise thee with the psaltery, I will sing to thy truth, O my God; unto thee will I sing with the harp, O thou Holy One of Israel.
71.23: My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.
71.24: My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long; for they are confounded, for they are brought to shame that seek my hurt.

72.1: GIVE the king thy judgments, O God, and thy righteousness to the king's son,
72.2: That he may judge thy people with righteousness and thy poor with justice.
72.3: The mountains shall bring peace to thy people, and the hills thy righteousness.
72.4: He shall judge the poor of the people; he shall save the children of the needy; and he shall crush the oppressor.
72.5: They shall revere thee as long as the sun and moon endure, throughout all generations.
72.6: He shall come down like rain upon the mown grass, as showers that water the earth.
72.7: In his days shall the righteous flourish, and abundance of peace so long as the moon endures.
72.8: He shall have dominion also from sea to sea, and from the river to the ends of the earth.
72.9: They that dwell on the islands shall bow before him; and his enemies shall lick the dust.
72.10: The kings of Tarshish and of the islands shall bring presents; the kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.
72.11: Yea, all kings shall worship him, all nations shall serve him;
72.12: For he shall deliver the needy from the mighty, the poor also, who has no helper.
72.13: He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.
72.14: He shall redeem their souls from deceit and violence; precious shall their blood be in his sight.
72.15: And he shall live, and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba; prayer also shall be made to him continually, and daily shall he be praised.
72.16: He shall multiply like wheat upon the earth; his seed shall spring up on the mountain tops, as on Lebanon; and they of the city shall flourish like grass of the earth.
72.17: His name shall endure for ever; his name shall be continued as long as the sun; and men shall be blessed in him; all nations shall call him blessed.
72.18: Blessed be the LORD God, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things.
72.19: And blessed be his glorious name for ever; and let the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen and Amen.

73.1: TRULY God is good to Israel, and to those who are pure in heart.
73.2: But as for me, my feet were almost gone; my steps had well nigh slipped.
73.3: For I was envious of the wicked when I saw the prosperity of the ungodly.
73.4: For there is no end to their death, and their folly is great.
73.5: They share not in the toil of men; neither are they scourged like other men.
73.6: Therefore pride holds them fast; they have concealed their wickedness and ungodliness.
73.7: Their iniquity comes through like grease; they do according to the evil dictates of the heart.
73.8: They imagine and speak evil things: they talk unjustly against the most High.
73.9: They have set their mouths in the sky, but their tongues drag in the dirt;
73.10: Therefore will my people return hither, and they shall have everything in abundance.
73.11: They shall say, How does God know? and is there knowledge in the most High?
73.12: Behold, these are the ungodly, who prosper in the world; they are strong in power.
73.13: As for me, I have cleansed my heart, and washed my hands in innocence.
73.14: All the day long have I been plagued, and chastened every morning.
73.15: If I say, I shall do as they do, this is sinful for me.
73.16: When I thought to know this, it was too painful for me.
73.17: Until I went into the sanctuary of God; then understood I their end.
73.18: Thou didst appoint their portion according to their deceitfulness; thou didst cast them down when they exalted themselves.
73.19: How are they brought into desolation, as in a moment! They are utterly consumed with terrors.
73.20: As one who awakes from a dream, so, O LORD, thou shalt despise their idolatry.
73.21: Thus my heart was grieved, and my conscience troubled me.
73.22: So foolish was I, and ignorant; I was as a beast before thee.
73.23: Thou shalt comfort me with thy counsel, and lead me according to thy honour.
73.24: Whom have I in heaven but thee? and whom have I desired upon earth besides thee?
73.25: Nevertheless I am continually with thee; thou hast held me by my right hand.
73.26: My flesh and my heart fail; but God is the strength of my heart and my portion for ever.
73.27: For lo, they that are far from thee shall perish; thou hast destroyed all them that go astray from thee.
73.28: I was pleased to draw near to God; I have put my trust in the LORD God, that I may declare all thy works.

74.1: O GOD, why hast thou cast us off for ever? Why hath thine anger become inflamed against the sheep of thy flock?
74.2: Remember thy congregation which thou hast possessed of old; the tribe of thine inheritance, which thou hast redeemed; this mount Zion, wherein thou hast dwelt.
74.3: Exalt thy servants over those who are carried away by power; all those who oppress are enemies of thy sanctuary.
74.4: Thine enemies exalted themselves in the midst of thy feasts; they set up their banners for signs.
74.5: Thou knowest this as the exalted one who sits on high: they have hewn down the doors with axes as they would cut the trees of the forest.
74.6: They have destroyed everything with axes and hammers.
74.7: They have set fire to and burned thy sanctuary; they have defiled the dwelling place of thy name on earth.
74.8: They said in their hearts, Let us destroy them together; let us abolish all the feast days of God from the land.
74.9: They did not see the wonders thereof; for there is no more any prophet, neither is there among us any wise man.
74.10: O God, how long shall the adversary reproach? Shall the enemy blaspheme thy name for ever?
74.11: Why withdrawest thou thy hand, even thy strength from the midst of thy congregation?
74.12: For God is our King, who of old has commanded the salvation of Jacob.
74.13: Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength: thou didst break the heads of the dragons in the waters.
74.14: Thou didst break the heads of Leviathan in pieces, and thou gavest him as food to a strong people.
74.15: Thou didst open the fountains in the valleys; thou didst dry up mighty rivers.
74.16: The day is thine, the night also is thine; thou hast prepared the light and the sun.
74.17: Thou hast set all the borders of the earth; thou hast made summer and winter.
74.18: Remember this, that the enemy has reproached, O LORD, and that the foolish people have blasphemed thy name.
74.19: O deliver not to destruction the soul that confesses to thee; forget not the souls of thy poor for ever.
74.20: Respect thy covenant, O LORD; for the habitations of the earth are full of ignorance and cruelty.
74.21: O let not the oppressed return ashamed; let the poor and needy praise thy name.
74.22: Arise, O God, plead thine own cause; remember how the foolish man reproacheth thee daily.
74.23: Forget not the voice of thine enemies; the tumult of those that rise up against thee ascendeth continually.

75.1: UNTO thee, O God, do we give thanks, unto thee do we give thanks and call upon thy name.
75.2: We will declare all thy wondrous works; then the time will come when I will judge uprightly.
75.3: Then the earth and all the inhabitants thereof shall be humbled; thou hast ordained the people thereof.
75.4: Thou saidst to the fools, Deal not foolishly, and to the wicked, Lift not up the horn;
75.5: Lift not up your horn on high; speak not with a stiff neck.
75.6: For defence comes neither from the west nor from the east nor from the desert mountains.
75.7: But God is the judge; he puts down one, and sets up another.
75.8: For in the hand of the LORD there is a cup, full of a mixture of the dregs of wine; and he passes it from one to another; surely all the wicked of the earth shall drink the dregs thereof.
75.9: But I will live for ever; I will sing praises to the God of Jacob.
75.10: All the horns of the wicked also will I cut off; but the horns of the righteous shall be exalted.

76.1: IN Judah is God known; his name is great in Israel.
76.2: In Salem also is his tabernacle, and his dwelling place in Zion.
76.3: There broke he the arms of the bow, the shield, and the sword, in the battle.
76.4: Thou art all glorious and excellent from thy mighty mountain.
76.5: All the foolish of heart were dismayed; the mighty men of valor slept their last sleep.
76.6: Their hands prevailed not because of thy rebuke, O God of Jacob; the horsemen are cast into a dead sleep.
76.7: Thou, even thou, art to be feared; who may stand in thy sight when once thou art angry?
76.8: Thou didst cause judgment to be heard from heaven; the earth saw it and trembled
76.9: When God arose to judgment, to save all the meek of the earth.
76.10: For the wisdom of man shall praise thee; the remainder of his wrath shalt thou restrain.
76.11: Vow and fulfil thy vows to the LORD thy God; let all that be round about him bring presents to him who is to be revered.
76.12: He shall humble the pride of the rulers; he is dreaded by the kings of the earth.

77.1: I CRIED to God with my voice, and he heard me; I have lifted up my voice to him, and he answered me.
77.2: In the day of my trouble I sought the LORD; my torment continued through the night, and ceased not; there was no comforter for my soul.
77.3: When I remembered, O God, I was troubled; I meditated, and my spirit was overwhelmed.
77.4: My eyes are dazed; I am dumb so that I cannot speak.
77.5: I have considered the days of old; I am mindful of the years of ancient times.
77.6: I meditated far into the night; I communed with mine own heart; I have examined my soul and said,
77.7: Has the LORD forsaken me for ever? and will he be favorable no more?
77.8: Is his mercy gone for ever? does his promise fail for evermore?
77.9: Has God forgotten to be gracious? has he in anger shut up his tender mercies?
77.10: And I said, This is my infirmity; it is another visitation of the power of the most High.
77.11: I will remember the works of the LORD; surely I will remember thy wonders of old.
77.12: I will meditate also of all thy works, and think of thy skillful doings.
77.13: Thy way, O God, is holy; there is none so great as our God.
77.14: Thou art the God that doest wonders; thou hast declared thy strength among the people.
77.15: Thou hast with thine arm saved thy people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph.
77.16: The waters saw thee, O God, the waters saw thee; they were afraid; the depths also were troubled.
77.17: The clouds poured out water; the skies sent out a sound; thine arrows also flew around.
77.18: The voice of thy thunder was in the heavens; the lightnings lightened the world; the earth trembled and shook.
77.19: Thy way is in the sea, and thy path is in the great waters, but thy footsteps are not seen.
77.20: Thou didst lead thy people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

78.1: GIVE ear, O my people, to my law; incline your ears to the words of my mouth.
78.2: I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter proverbs of old.
78.3: That which we have heard and known, and which our fathers have told us,
78.4: We will not hide from their children, showing to the generation to come the praise of the LORD and his strength and the wonderful works that he has done.
78.5: For he established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers that they should make known to their children,
78.6: That the generation to come might know them, even the children which should be born, who should arise and declare them to their children;
78.7: That they might set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments;
78.8: And might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation, a generation that set not their heart aright, and whose spirit was not steadfast with God.
78.9: The children of Ephraim, being armed and throwing forth bows, turned back in the day of battle.
78.10: For they kept not the covenant of God, and refused to walk in his law;
78.11: And forgot his works and his wonders that he had shown them in the sight of their fathers.
78.12: Marvellous things did he in the land of Egypt and in the fields of Zoan.
78.13: He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as in skins.
78.14: In the daytime also he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a light of fire.
78.15: He cleaved the rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink as out of great depths.
78.16: He brought forth streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.
78.17: But the people continued to sin, and to murmur against the most High in their thirst for water.
78.18: And they tempted God in their hearts by asking food to satisfy their desires.
78.19: Yea, they complained against God; they said, Can God furnish tables of food in the wilderness?
78.20: Behold, he smote the rock, that the waters gushed out and the streams overflowed; can he give bread also? can he provide food for his people?
78.21: Therefore the LORD heard this and was angered; so a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also came up against Israel,
78.22: Because they believed not in God and trusted not in his salvation:
78.23: Though he had commanded the clouds from above and opened the doors of heaven,
78.24: And had rained down manna for them to eat and had given them of the bread of heaven.
78.25: Man did eat angels' food; he sent them game in abundance.
78.26: He caused winds to blow in the skies; and by his power he brought in the south wind.
78.27: He rained flesh also upon them like dust, and feathered fowls like the sands of the sea:
78.28: And he let these fall in the midst of their camp, round about their tents.
78.29: So they did eat and were well filled; for he gave them their own desire.
78.30: They relinquished not their craving. But while their food was yet in their mouths,
78.31: The anger of God came upon them and slew the richest of them, and made to bow the chosen men of Israel.
78.32: Nevertheless they sinned still, and believed not in his wondrous works.
78.33: Wherefore their days were spent in emptiness, and their years in impatience.
78.34: When he slew them, then they sought him; and they returned and drew nearer to him.
78.35: And they remembered that God was their helper, and the high God their Saviour.
78.36: Nevertheless they loved him with their mouths, and they lied to him with their tongues.
78.37: For their hearts were not right with him, neither were they steadfast in his covenant.
78.38: He is merciful, the forgiver of sins, and destroys not; yea, many a time turned he his anger away and did not stir up all his wrath.
78.39: For he remembered that they were but flesh; a wind that passes away and comes not again.
78.40: How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness, and grieve him in the desert!
78.41: Yea, they turned back and tempted God, and defied the Holy One of Israel.
78.42: They remembered not his hand, nor the day when he delivered them from the oppressor,
78.43: How he had wrought his signs in Egypt and his wonders in the field of Zoan,
78.44: Nor how he had turned their rivers into blood and their brooks, that they could not drink.
78.45: He sent swarms of insects among them, which devoured them, and frogs, which destroyed them.
78.46: He gave also their crops to the locusts, and their labor to the caterpillars.
78.47: He destroyed their vines with hail and their fig trees with frost.
78.48: He gave up their cattle also to the hail and their flocks to destruction.
78.49: He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, indignation, and trouble, by the hand of an evil angel.
78.50: He resorted to strong measures; he spared not their souls from death, and gave their cattle over to the pestilence;
78.51: And smote all the first-born in Egypt, the fairest of their sons in the tabernacles of Ham;
78.52: But he led forth his own people like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.
78.53: And he led them on safely so that they feared not; but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.
78.54: And he brought them to the border of his sanctuary, even to this mountain, which his right hand had possessed.
78.55: He cast out the Gentiles from before them, and allotted them an inheritance by measure, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.
78.56: Yet they tempted and provoked the most High God, and kept not his testimonies;
78.57: But turned back and dealt deceitfully like their fathers; they were crooked like a twisted bow.
78.58: For they provoked him to anger by sacrificing on high places, and made him indignant with their graven images.
78.59: When God heard this, he was wroth, and greatly abhorred Israel,
78.60: So that he forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men;
78.61: And he delivered his people into captivity, and his glory into the oppressor's hand.
78.62: He delivered his people to the sword, and disregarded his inheritance.
78.63: The fire consumed their young men; and their maidens were violated.
78.64: Their priests fell by the sword; and their widows made no lamentation.
78.65: Then the LORD awaked as one out of sleep and like a mighty man who has shaken off the effects of wine.
78.66: And he caused his enemies to retreat; he put them to a perpetual reproach.
78.67: Moreover he refused the tabernacle of Joseph, and chose not the tribe of Ephraim;
78.68: But chose the tribe of Judah, mount Zion which he loved.
78.69: And he built his sanctuary on a high place, and he established it upon the earth for ever.
78.70: He chose David also his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds;
78.71: From following the ewes that give suck, he brought him to feed Jacob his people and Israel his inheritance.
78.72: So he shepherded them according to the integrity of his heart; and guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.

79.1: GOD, the heathen are come into thine inheritance; thy holy temple have they defiled; they have laid Jerusalem in heaps.
79.2: The dead bodies of thy servants have they given to be meat to the fowls of the heavens, the flesh of thy saints to the beasts of the earth.
79.3: Their blood have they shed like water round about Jerusalem; and there was none to bury them.
79.4: We are become a reproach to our neighbours, a scorn and derision to them that are round about us.
79.5: How long, O LORD? wilt thou be angry for ever? shall thy anger burn like fire?
79.6: Pour out thy wrath upon the heathen that have not known thee, and upon the kingdoms that have not called upon thy name.
79.7: For they have devoured Jacob, and laid waste his dwelling place.
79.8: O remember not against us our former sins; let thy tender mercies speedily overtake us; for we are brought very low.
79.9: Help us, O God of our salvation, for the glory of thy name; and deliver us and purge away our sins, for thy name's sake.
79.10: Wherefore should the heathen say, Where is their God? Let him be known among the heathen in our sight by the revenging of the blood of thy servants which is shed.
79.11: Let the sighing of the prisoner come before thee; according to the greatness of thy power, free thou those that are appointed to die.
79.12: And render to our neighbours sevenfold into their bosom their reproach, wherewith they have reproached thee, O LORD.
79.13: So we thy people and sheep of thy pasture will give thee thanks for ever; we will tell thy wonders to all generations.

80.1: GIVE ear, O Shepherd of Israel, thou that leadest Joseph like a flock; thou that sittest upon the cherubim, shine forth.
80.2: Before Ephraim and Benjamin and Manasseh show forth thy strength and come and save us.
80.3: Guide us again, O mighty God, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
80.4: O LORD God of hosts, how long wilt thou be angry against the prayer of thy people?
80.5: Thou feedest them with the bread of tears; and givest them tears to drink in great measure.
80.6: Thou hast made us a jest to our neighbors; and our enemies laugh among themselves.
80.7: Guide us again, O God of hosts, and cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.
80.8: Thou hast brought a vine out of Egypt; thou hast cast out the heathen and planted it.
80.9: Thou didst prepare the ground and caused it to take deep root, and it filled the land.
80.10: The mountains were covered with the shadow of it, and the vine-shoots thereof were upon the cedars of God.
80.11: It sent out its roots unto the sea, and its branches to the rivers.
80.12: Why hast thou then broken down its hedges, so that all they who pass by the way tread over it?
80.13: The boar of the forest devours it, and the wild beasts of the field feed upon it.
80.14: Return we beseech thee, O God of hosts; look down from heaven, and behold, and visit this vine
80.15: And the vineyard which thy right hand has planted and the branch that thou madest strong for thyself.
80.16: It is burned with fire; it is cut down; they perish at the rebuke of thy countenance.
80.17: Let thy right hand be upon the man and upon the son of man whom thou madest strong for thyself.
80.18: So will we not go back from thee; quicken us, and we will call upon thy name.
80.19: Guide us again, O LORD God of hosts, cause thy face to shine; and we shall be saved.

81.1: SING aloud to God our strength; make a joyful noise to the God of Jacob.
81.2: Take a psalm and bring hither the timbrel, the pleasant harp with the psaltery.
81.3: Blow trumpets in the new moon in the time appointed, on our solemn feast days.
81.4: For this was a statute for Israel and a law of the God of Jacob.
81.5: This he ordained in Joseph for a testimony when he went out to the land of Egypt, where he heard a language that he understood not.
81.6: I removed the yoke from his shoulder, I released his hands from the bonds.
81.7: He called on me in trouble, and I delivered him; I sheltered him under my glorious cover; I tested him at the waters of dispute.
81.8: Hear, O my people, and I will speak; O Israel, I will testify about you. If you will listen to me;
81.9: There shall be no strange god in you; neither shall you worship any strange god.
81.10: I am the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt; open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.
81.11: But my people would not listen to my voice; and Israel would have none of me.
81.12: So they walked according to the desires of their own hearts and according to their own counsels.
81.13: Oh that my people had listened to me, and Israel had walked in my ways!
81.14: I would soon have destroyed their enemies, and turned my hand against their adversaries.
81.15: The haters of the LORD have lied to him; but they shall tremble for ever.
81.16: He has fed them also with the finest of the wheat; and with honey out of the rock has he satisfied them.

82.1: GOD stands in the congregation of angels; he judges among the angels.
82.2: How long will you judge unjustly, and be partial to the wicked?
82.3: Defend the poor and fatherless; do justice to the afflicted and needy.
82.4: Deliver the poor and needy out of the hand of the wicked.
82.5: They know not, neither will they understand; they walk on in darkness; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
82.6: I have said, You are gods; all of you are children of the most High.
82.7: Henceforth you shall die like men, and fall like one of the princes.
82.8: Arise, O God, judge the earth: for thou shalt inherit all nations.

83.1: O GOD, who is like unto thee? Hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God;
83.2: For, lo, thine enemies are raging and they that hate thee have lifted up the head against thy people.
83.3: They have taken crafty counsel, and consulted against thy chosen ones.
83.4: They have said, Come, let us destroy them from among the nations, that the name of Israel may be no more in remembrance.
83.5: For they have agreed with one heart; they have made alliance against thee:
83.6: The tabernacles of Edom and the Ishmaelites; of Moab and the Gadarenes;
83.7: The people of the region of Ammon and Amalek; the Philistines with the inhabitants of Tyre;
83.8: The Assyrians have also joined with them; they have helped the children of Lot.
83.9: Do to them as to the Midianites; as to Sisera, as to Jabin, at the valley of Kishon;
83.10: Which perished at Endor; they became as manure for the earth.
83.11: Destroy their nobles like Oreb and like Zeeb; yea, all their princes as Zebah and as Zalmunna;
83.12: For they said, Let us inherit for ourselves the city of God.
83.13: O God, make them like a turning wheel; and like stubble before the wind.
83.14: As the fire burns a forest and as the flame sets the mountains on fire;
83.15: So persecute them with thy tempest, and make them afraid with thy storm.
83.16: Fill their faces with shame, that they may seek thy name, O LORD.
83.17: Let them be confounded and troubled for ever; yea, let them be put to shame, and perish,
83.18: That men may know that thou, whose name alone is the LORD, art the most high over all the earth.

84.1: HOW amiable are thy tabernacles, O LORD of hosts!
84.2: My soul longs, yea, even faints for the courts of the LORD; my heart and my flesh give praise for the living God.
84.3: Yea, even the sparrow has found a house, and the pigeon a nest for herself, where they have raised their young beside thine altars, O LORD of hosts, my King, and my God.
84.4: Blessed are they that dwell in thy house; they shall praise thee for ever.
84.5: Blessed is the man whose help is from thee; in whose heart are thy ways.
84.6: They have passed through the valley of weeping, and have made it a dwelling place; the Lawgiver shall cover it with blessings;
84.7: They shall go from strength to strength; the God of gods shall be seen in Zion.
84.8: O LORD God of hosts, hear my prayer; give ear, O God of Jacob.
84.9: Behold, O God our shield, and look upon the face of thine anointed.
84.10: For a day in thy courts is better than a thousand. I would choose rather to dwell in the house of my God than to dwell in the tents of the wicked.
84.11: For the LORD God is our supply and our helper; the LORD will give grace and glory; no good thing will he withhold from them that walk uprightly.
84.12: O LORD of hosts, blessed is the man who trusts in thee.

85.1: LORD, thou hast been favorable to thy land; thou hast brought back the captivity of Jacob.
85.2: Thou hast forgiven the iniquity of thy people, thou hast covered all their sin.
85.3: Thou hast taken away all thy wrath; thou hast withdrawn the fierceness of thine anger.
85.4: Restore us, O God our Saviour, and cause thine anger toward us to cease.
85.5: Be not angry with us for ever; hold not thine anger against all generations.
85.6: Restore us and revive us, that thy people may rejoice in thee.
85.7: Show us thy mercy, O LORD, and grant us thy salvation.
85.8: We will hear what the LORD our God will speak; for he will speak peace to his people and to his saints, that they may not turn again to folly.
85.9: Surely his salvation is near them that reverence him; let his glory dwell in our land.
85.10: Mercy and truth are met together; righteousness and peace have kissed each other.
85.11: Truth shall spring out of the earth; and righteousness shall look down from heaven.
85.12: Yea, the LORD shall give his goodness; and the land shall yield its fruits.
85.13: And the righteous shall go before him; and he shall establish his ways upon the earth.

86.1: INCLINE thine ear, O LORD; hear me, for I am poor and needy.
86.2: Preserve my soul, for thou art holy; O thou God, save thy servant who trusts in thee.
86.3: Be merciful to me, O LORD; for I cry unto thee daily.
86.4: Rejoice the soul of thy servant; for unto thee, O LORD, do I lift up my soul.
86.5: For thou, O LORD, art good; and thy lovingkindness is abundant to all who call upon thee.
86.6: Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer; and attend to the voice of my supplications.
86.7: In the day of my trouble I have called upon thee, and thou hast answered me.
86.8: There is none like thee, O LORD my God; neither are there any works like thy works.
86.9: All the nations whom thou hast made shall come and worship before thee, O LORD, and shall glorify thy name.
86.10: For thou art great, and doest wondrous things; thou art God alone.
86.11: Show me thy way, O LORD; I will walk in the truth; my heart will rejoice with them that worship thy name.
86.12: I will praise thee, O LORD my God, with all my heart; and I will glorify thy name for evermore.
86.13: For great is thy lovingkindness toward me; and thou hast delivered my soul from the depths of hell.
86.14: O God, the wicked are risen against me, and the assembly of violent men has sought after my soul; and they have not been mindful of thee.
86.15: But thou, O LORD God, art full of compassion and gracious, longsuffering and plenteous in mercy and truth.
86.16: O turn to me and have mercy upon me; give strength to thy servant, and save the son of thy handmaid.
86.17: Show me a token for good, that they which hate me may see it and be ashamed, because thou, LORD, hast helped me and comforted me.

87.1: HER foundations are on his holy mountain.
87.2: The LORD loves the gates of Zion more than all the dwellings of Jacob.
87.3: Glorious things are spoken of you, O city of our God.
87.4: I will make mention of Rahab and Babylon, for they know my power: behold the Philistines and Tyre and the people of Ethiopia; it shall be said this man was born there.
87.5: And of Zion it shall be said, This mighty man was born in her; and the same shall establish her.
87.6: The LORD shall number his people in the book: This man was born there!
87.7: The princes who dwell in you shall rejoice, and all that are humbled in you.

88.1: O LORD God of my salvation, I have cried day and night before thee;
88.2: Let my prayer come before thee; incline thine ear unto my supplication;
88.3: For my soul is full of troubles and my life draws near to Sheol.
88.4: I am counted with them that go down into the pit; I am like a man who has no strength;
88.5: Like a nobleman abandoned among the dead, like the slain that lie down in the grave, whom thou rememberest no more; they are lost to thee.
88.6: Thou hast laid me in the lowest pit, in darkness, in the shadow of death.
88.7: Thy wrath rests hard upon me, and thou hast afflicted me with all thy waves.
88.8: Thou hast put away mine acquaintances far from me; thou hast made me an abomination to them; I am shut up and I cannot come forth.
88.9: Mine eyes are weakened from weeping; LORD, I have called daily upon thee; I have stretched out my hands to thee.
88.10: Behold thou wilt show wonders to the dead; the mighty ones shall rise and praise thee.
88.11: They that are in the graves shall declare thy lovingkindness, and thy faithfulness in destruction.
88.12: Thy wonders shall be known in the dark, and thy righteousness in the land that has been forsaken.
88.13: But to thee have I cried, O LORD; and in the morning shall my prayer come early before thee.
88.14: O LORD, forsake not my soul, and turn not thy face from me.
88.15: I am poor and afflicted from my youth; I have been proud, but now I am humbled and crushed.
88.16: Thy fierce wrath goes over me; thy terrors have silenced me.
88.17: They came round about me daily like water; they set themselves in array against me.
88.18: Friends and neighbors thou hast put far from me, and mine acquaintances thou hast kept away from me.

89.1: I WILL sing of the mercies of the LORD for ever; with my mouth will I make known thy faithfulness to all generations.
89.2: For I have said, The world shall be built up with mercy; thy faithfulness shalt thou establish in the very heavens.
89.3: I have made a covenant with my chosen, I have sworn to David my servant,
89.4: Thy offspring will I establish for ever, and build up thy throne to all generations.
89.5: And the heavens shall praise thy wonders, O LORD, thy faithfulness also in the congregation of the saints.
89.6: For who in heaven can be compared to the LORD? who among the angels can be likened to the LORD?
89.7: God stands forth in the assembly of the saints, great and revered above all that are about him.
89.8: O LORD God of hosts, who is mighty like thee? and thy faithfulness is round about thee.
89.9: Thou rulest the splendor of the sea; thou stillest the waves thereof.
89.10: Thou hast humbled the proud as those that are slain; thou hast scattered thine enemies with thy strong arm.
89.11: The heavens are thine, the earth also is thine; as for the world and the fulness thereof, thou hast founded them.
89.12: The north and the south, thou hast created them; Tabor and Hermon glorify thy name.
89.13: Thine is the arm, and thine is the might; thy hand is strong and thy right hand shall be exalted.
89.14: Thy throne is built on righteousness and judgment; mercy and truth go before thy face.
89.15: Blessed is the people that understand thy glory; they shall walk, O LORD, in the light of thy countenance.
89.16: In thy name shall they rejoice all the day; and in thy righteousness shall they be exalted.
89.17: For thou art the glory of our strength; and in thy favor our horn shall be exalted.
89.18: For the LORD is our hope; and the Holy One of Israel is our King.
89.19: Then he spoke in visions to his righteous one, and he said, I have given help to a man; I have exalted one chosen out of the people.
89.20: I have found David my servant; with my holy oil have I anointed him.
89.21: My hand has helped him; mine arm also has strengthened him.
89.22: The enemy shall not prevail upon him, nor the sons of the wicked afflict him.
89.23: And I will destroy his foes before his face, and plague them that hate him.
89.24: But my faithfulness and my mercy shall be with him; and in my name shall his horn be exalted.
89.25: I will set his hand also in the sea, and his right hand in the rivers.
89.26: He shall cry to me, Thou art my father, my God, and my mighty Saviour.
89.27: Also I will make him my first-born, and will exalt him over the kings of the earth.
89.28: My mercy will I keep for him for evermore, and my covenant shall stand fast with him.
89.29: His offspring also will I make to endure for ever, and his throne as the days of heaven.
89.30: If his children forsake my law and walk not in my judgments;
89.31: If they break my statutes and keep not my commandments;
89.32: Then will I visit their transgression with the rod, and their iniquity with stripes.
89.33: Nevertheless my lovingkindness will I not utterly take from him, nor suffer my faithfulness to fail.
89.34: Neither will I reject my covenant, nor alter the thing that is gone out of my lips.
89.35: Once have I sworn by my holiness to David, and I will not lie.
89.36: His offspring shall endure for ever, and his throne as the sun before me.
89.37: It shall be established for ever as the moon, and as a faithful witness in heaven.
89.38: But thou hast forsaken us and despised us, thou hast made thine anointed one to turn his face.
89.39: Thou hast rejected the covenant of thy servant; thou hast cast his crown to the ground.
89.40: Thou hast broken down all his hedges; thou hast brought his strongholds to ruin.
89.41: All that pass by have trampled upon him; he has become a reproach to his neighbours.
89.42: Thou hast set up the right hand of his adversaries; thou hast made all his enemies to rejoice.
89.43: Thou hast turned the edge of his sword, and hast not sustained him in the battle.
89.44: Thou hast made his glory to cease, and cast his throne down to the ground.
89.45: The days of his youth hast thou shortened; thou hast covered him with shame.
89.46: How long, LORD, wilt thou be angry? for ever? shall thy wrath burn like fire?
89.47: Remember me from the time I was created; for thou hast not created all men in vain.
89.48: Who is the man who lives and shall not see death? Shall he deliver his soul from the hand of Sheol?
89.49: LORD, where are thy former lovingkindnesses, which thou didst swear to David in thy truth?
89.50: Remember, LORD, the reproach of thy servants; for I have borne throughout my life the mockery of the Gentiles;
89.51: Wherewith thine enemies have reproached me, O LORD; wherewith they have reproached the footsteps of thine anointed.
89.52: Blessed be the LORD for evermore. Amen and Amen.

90.1: LORD, thou hast been our dwelling place in all generations.
90.2: Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever thou hadst formed the earth and the world, even from everlasting to everlasting, thou art God.
90.3: Thou turnest man to destruction; and sayest, Return, ye children of men.
90.4: For a thousand years in thy sight are but as yesterday when it is past, and as a watch in the night.
90.5: The span of their life will be as a sleep; in the morning they are like grass which changes.
90.6: In the morning it flourishes and grows up; in the evening it is cut down and withers.
90.7: For we are consumed by thine anger, and by thy wrath are we troubled.
90.8: Thou hast set our iniquities before thee, the sins of our youth in the light of thy countenance.
90.9: For all our days are passed away in thy wrath; we spend our years in emptiness.
90.10: The years of our lives are threescore and ten; and if by reason of strength they be fourscore years, yet most of them are labor and sorrow; for life is soon cut off and we fly away.
90.11: Who knows the power of thine anger and the fear of thy wrath?
90.12: So teach us to number our days that we may apply our hearts to wisdom.
90.13: Return, O LORD. How long? Wouldst thou not comfort thy servants?
90.14: O satisfy us early with thy mercy, that we may rejoice and be glad all our days.
90.15: Make us glad because our wickedness is dead, and the years wherein we have seen affliction.
90.16: Let thy work appear to thy servants, and thy glory to their children.
90.17: And let the beauty of the LORD our God be upon us; for the work of his hands made us, yea, he made us by the work of his hands.

91.1: HE who dwells in the protection of the most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty.
91.2: I will say of the LORD, He is my refuge and my fortress; my God; in him will I trust.
91.3: Surely he shall deliver you from the snare of the fowler, and from vain gossip.
91.4: He will cover you with his feathers, and under his wings you shall trust; his truth shall be your shield and buckler.
91.5: You shall not be afraid for the terror by night, nor for the arrow that flies by day,
91.6: Nor for the conspiracy that spreads in darkness; nor for the pestilence that wastes at noonday.
91.7: Thousands shall fall at your side, and ten thousand at your right hand; but it shall not come near you.
91.8: Only with your eyes shall you behold the reward of the wicked.
91.9: For thou, O LORD, art my trust; thou hast established thy habitation in the highest.
91.10: There shall no evil befall you, neither shall any plague come near your dwelling.
91.11: For he shall give his angels charge over you to keep you in all your ways.
91.12: They shall bear you up in their hands, lest you dash your foot against a stone.
91.13: You shall tread upon the viper and adder; you shall trample under foot the lion and the great serpent.
91.14: Because he has loved me, therefore will I deliver him; I will set him on high because he has known my name.
91.15: He shall call upon me, and I will answer him; I will be with him in trouble; I will deliver him and honor him.
91.16: With long life will I satisfy him, and show him my salvation.

92.1: It is a good thing to give thanks to the LORD, and to sing praises to thy name, O Most High,
92.2: To show forth thy lovingkindness in the morning, and thy faithfulness every night.
92.3: I will play upon an instrument of ten strings and upon the psaltery; upon the harp with a solemn sound.
92.4: For thou, O LORD, hast made me glad through thy work; I will triumph in the works of thy hands.
92.5: O LORD, how great are thy works! and thy thoughts are very deep.
92.6: A stupid man knows not; neither does a fool understand this.
92.7: When the wicked spring as the grass, and when all the workers of iniquity flourish; it is that they shall be destroyed for ever;
92.8: But thou, LORD, art most high for evermore.
92.9: For, lo, thine enemies, O LORD, for lo, thine enemies shall perish; all the workers of iniquity shall be scattered.
92.10: But my horn shalt thou exalt like the horn of a wild ox; I shall be anointed with fragrant oil.
92.11: Mine eyes also shall see my desire on mine enemies, and mine ears shall hear my desire against the wicked that rise up against me.
92.12: The righteous shall flourish like the palm tree; he shall grow like a cedar in Lebanon.
92.13: Those that are planted in the house of the LORD shall flourish in the courts of our God.
92.14: They shall still bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be fair and desirable,
92.15: To show that the LORD is upright; he is mighty, and there is no unrighteousness in him.

93.1: THE LORD reigns; he is clothed with majesty; the LORD is clothed with strength, wherewith he has girded himself; the world also is established, that it cannot be moved.
93.2: Thy throne is established of old; thou art from everlasting.
93.3: The rivers are full flowing, O LORD; the rivers have lifted up their voice; the rivers are flowing with purity.
93.4: The LORD on high is mightier than the noise of many waters, yea, than the mighty waves of the sea.
93.5: Thy testimonies are very sure; holiness becomes thy house, O LORD, for ever.

94.1: O LORD God, to whom vengeance belongs, O God, to whom vengeance belongs, show thyself.
94.2: Lift up thyself, thou judge of the earth; render a reward to the proud.
94.3: LORD, how long shall the wicked, how long shall the wicked boast?
94.4: They shall utter and speak unjust things; they shall speak all kinds of evil things.
94.5: For they have humbled thy people, O LORD, and subdued thine heritage.
94.6: They slay the widow and the innocent, and murder the fatherless.
94.7: Yet they say, The LORD does not see, neither does the God of Jacob regard it.
94.8: Understand, you stupid among the people and you fools, when will you be wise?
94.9: He who planted the ear, shall he not hear? He who formed the eye, shall he not see?
94.10: He who chastises the nations, shall not he rebuke? He who teaches man knowledge, shall he not know?
94.11: The LORD knows the thoughts of man; they are like a breath.
94.12: Blessed is the man whom thou dost chasten, O LORD, and teachest out of thy law;
94.13: That thou mayest give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit be digged for the wicked.
94.14: For the LORD will not cast off his people, neither will he forsake his inheritance.
94.15: For judgment shall return to the righteous, and all the upright in heart shall follow it.
94.16: Who will rise up for me against the evildoers? or who will stand up against the workers of iniquity?
94.17: Unless the LORD had been my help, my soul would soon have been in trouble.
94.18: When I said, My foot slips, thy mercy, O LORD, held me up.
94.19: In the multitude of my sorrows, within my heart thy comforts delight my soul.
94.20: Evil rulers shall not have fellowship with thee, for they have framed mischief against thy law.
94.21: They lay snares to trap the soul of the righteous, and condemn the innocent blood.
94.22: But the LORD is my defense; and my God is the rock of my refuge.
94.23: And he shall bring upon them their own iniquity, and shall silence them in their own wickedness; yea, the LORD our God shall silence them.

95.1: O COME, let us sing to the LORD; let us make a joyful noise to the God of our salvation.
95.2: Let us come before his presence with thanksgiving, and make a joyful noise to him with psalms.
95.3: For the LORD is a great God and a great King above all gods.
95.4: In his hand are the foundations of the earth; the strength of the hills is his also.
95.5: The sea is his, and he made it; and his hands formed the dry land.
95.6: O come, let us worship and bow down; let us kneel before the LORD our Maker.
95.7: For he is our God; and we are the people of his pasture and the sheep of his hand. Today if you will hear his voice,
95.8: Harden not your heart, as in the provocation and as in the day of temptation in the wilderness,
95.9: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my work.
95.10: Forty years long was I grieved with that generation, and said, It is a people that do err in their heart, and they have not known my ways,
95.11: Unto whom I swore in my wrath that they should not enter into my rest.

96.1: O SING to the LORD a new song; sing to the LORD, all the earth.
96.2: Sing to the LORD, bless his name; show forth his salvation from day to day.
96.3: Declare his glory among the heathen, his wonders among all people.
96.4: For the LORD is great and greatly to be praised; he is to be revered above all gods.
96.5: For all the gods of the nations are idols; but the LORD made the heavens.
96.6: Honor and majesty are before him; strength and beauty are in his sanctuary.
96.7: Give to the LORD, O you kindred of the people, give to the LORD glory and honor.
96.8: Give to the LORD the glory due to his name; bring an offering and come into his courts.
96.9: O worship the LORD in the temple of holiness; tremble before him, all the earth.
96.10: Say among the Gentiles that the LORD reigns; the world also shall be established that it shall not be moved; he shall judge the people righteously.
96.11: Let the heavens rejoice, and let the earth be glad; let the sea roar and the fulness thereof.
96.12: Let the field be joyful, and all that is therein; then shall all the trees of the forest rejoice
96.13: Before the LORD; for he comes, for he comes to judge the earth; he shall judge the world with righteousness, and the people with his truth.

97.1: THE LORD reigns; let the earth rejoice; let the multitude of isles be glad thereof.
97.2: Clouds and darkness are round about him; righteousness and judgment are the foundation of his throne.
97.3: Fire goes before him, and burns up his enemies round about.
97.4: His lightnings enlightened the world; the earth saw, and trembled.
97.5: The hills melted like wax at the presence of the LORD, at the presence of the LORD of the whole earth.
97.6: The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the people see his glory.
97.7: Confounded are all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols; worship him, all his angels.
97.8: Zion heard and was glad; and the daughters of Judah rejoiced because of thy judgments, O LORD.
97.9: For thou, LORD, art high above all the earth; thou art exalted far above all gods.
97.10: You who love the LORD, hate evil; he preserves the souls of his saints; he delivers them out of the hand of the wicked.
97.11: Light shone for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.
97.12: Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous; and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

98.1: SING to the LORD a new song; for he has done marvellous things; his right hand and his holy arm have gotten him the victory.
98.2: The LORD has made known his salvation; his righteousness has he revealed in the sight of the heathen.
98.3: He has remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel; all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
98.4: Praise the LORD, all the earth; make ye merry and sing and give praise.
98.5: Sing to the LORD with the harp, with the harp and the voice of a choir.
98.6: With the sound of trumpets give praise before the LORD, the King.
98.7: Let the sea roar and the fulness thereof, the world and they that dwell therein.
98.8: Let the rivers clap their hands; let the hills be joyful together
98.9: Before the LORD; for he comes to judge the earth; with righteousness shall he judge the world, and the people with equity.

99.1: THE LORD reigns; let the people tremble; he sits upon the cherubim; let the earth be moved.
99.2: The LORD is great in Zion; he is high above all the people.
99.3: Let them praise thy great and honored name; for it is holy.
99.4: The king's might also loves judgment; thou dost establish equity, thou executest judgment and righteousness in Jacob.
99.5: Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his footstool; for he is holy.
99.6: Moses and Aaron were among his priests, and Samuel among them that call upon his name; they called upon the LORD, and he answered them.
99.7: He spoke to them in the cloudy pillar; they kept his testimonies and the covenant that he gave them.
99.8: Thou didst answer them, O LORD our God; thou wast an avenger for them. O God, reward them according to their works.
99.9: Exalt the LORD our God, and worship at his holy mountain; for the LORD our God is holy.

100.1: MAKE a joyful noise to the LORD, all you lands.
100.2: Serve the LORD with gladness; come before his presence with singing.
100.3: Know that he is the LORD our God; it is he who has made us, and not we ourselves; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.
100.4: Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, and into his courts with praise; be thankful to him and bless his name;
100.5: For the LORD is good; his mercy is everlasting; and his truth endures to all generations.

101.1: I WILL sing of mercy and judgment; unto thee, O LORD, will I sing.
101.2: I will behave myself wisely in a perfect way. O when wilt thou come to me? I will walk within my house with a perfect heart.
101.3: I will set no wicked thing before mine eyes; I hate the doers of evil, they shall not come near to me.
101.4: A perverse heart shall depart from me; I will know not evil.
101.5: He who accuses his neighbor falsely will I destroy; with him who has a high look and a proud heart I will not eat.
101.6: Mine eyes shall be upon the faithful of the land, that they may dwell with me; he who walks in a perfect way shall serve me.
101.7: He who works deceit shall not dwell within my house; he who tells lies shall not tarry in my sight.
101.8: In the early morning I will silence all the wicked of the earth; and I will destroy all the doers of iniquity from the city of the LORD.

102.1: HEAR my prayer, O LORD, and let my cry come unto thee.
102.2: Turn not thy face from me in the day when I am in trouble; incline thine ear unto me; in the day when I call, answer me speedily.
102.3: For my days are consumed like smoke, and my bones are whitened as if they were burned.
102.4: My heart is smitten and withered like grass, so that I forget to eat my bread.
102.5: Because of the voice of my groaning, my bones cleave to my skin.
102.6: I am like a pelican of the wilderness; I am like an owl of the desert.
102.7: I am shaken and alone like a sparrow upon the house tops.
102.8: Mine enemies reproach me all the day; those who once praised me are now sworn against me.
102.9: I have eaten ashes like bread, and mingled my drink with weeping,
102.10: Because of thine indignation and thy wrath; for thou hast lifted me up, and cast me down.
102.11: My days are like a shadow that declines; and I am withered like grass.
102.12: But thou, O LORD, shalt endure for ever, and thy remembrance to all generations.
102.13: Arise and have mercy upon Zion; for it is time to have mercy upon her.
102.14: For thy servants take pleasure in her stones, and favor the dust thereof.
102.15: So the heathen shall worship the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth thy glory.
102.16: When the LORD builds up Zion, he shall appear in his glory.
102.17: He will regard the prayer of the destitute, and not despise their prayer.
102.18: This shall be written for the generation to come; and the people who shall be created shall praise the LORD.
102.19: For he has looked down from the height of his sanctuary; from heaven did the LORD behold the earth,
102.20: To hear the groaning of the prisoner, to loose those that are appointed to death,
102.21: To declare the name of the LORD in Zion and his praises in Jerusalem,
102.22: When the peoples are gathered together, and the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
102.23: For they have weakened my strength on earth; they warned me of the shortage of my days.
102.24: O my God, take me not away in the midst of my days; thy years are throughout all generations.
